trackers.name: Office of the Colorado State Public Defender
trackers.desc: Statewide public defender representing anyone accused of a crime who cannot afford counsel — 21 regional trial offices across 23 judicial districts, plus an Appellate Division in Denver.

trackers.name: Colorado Office of the Alternate Defense Counsel
trackers.desc: Provides court-appointed counsel in Colorado cases where the State Public Defender has a conflict of interest.

trackers.name: Connecticut Division of Public Defender Services
trackers.desc: Statewide indigent defense agency, including a Capital Defense and Trial Services Unit and the Connecticut Innocence Project / Post-Conviction Unit.

trackers.name: Delaware Office of the Public Defender
trackers.desc: Statewide public defender offices providing criminal defense to people who cannot afford an attorney.

trackers.name: Iowa State Public Defender
trackers.desc: State agency providing legal representation to indigent people charged with crimes in Iowa.

trackers.name: Kansas State Board of Indigents’ Defense Services
trackers.desc: Oversees and provides indigent criminal defense across Kansas.

trackers.name: Kentucky Department of Public Advocacy
trackers.desc: Kentucky’s statewide public defender agency — also houses the Kentucky Innocence Project and a Capital Post-Conviction Unit.

trackers.name: Louisiana Public Defender Board
trackers.desc: Oversees Louisiana’s district public defender offices and statewide indigent defense delivery.

trackers.name: Mississippi Office of the State Public Defender
trackers.desc: Statewide public defender office serving people charged with crimes who cannot afford counsel.

trackers.name: Mississippi Office of Capital Post-Conviction Counsel
trackers.desc: Represents people sentenced to death in Mississippi during post-conviction proceedings.

trackers.name: Missouri Office of the State Public Defender
trackers.desc: Missouri’s statewide public defender system providing trial, appellate and capital representation.

trackers.name: West Virginia Public Defender Services
trackers.desc: State agency administering public defender corporations and appointed counsel across West Virginia.

trackers.name: Wisconsin State Public Defender
trackers.desc: Statewide public defender with offices across Wisconsin, providing trial and appellate representation.

trackers.name: Neighborhood Justice Center
trackers.desc: Saint Paul nonprofit providing holistic criminal defense and support to low-income clients.

trackers.name: Legal Rights Center
trackers.desc: Minneapolis community-based public defense and restorative-justice organisation.

trackers.name: Minnesota Board of Public Defense
trackers.desc: Oversees Minnesota’s statewide and district public defender offices.

trackers.name: Public Defender Service for the District of Columbia
trackers.desc: Independent federally funded defender organisation representing indigent people in DC criminal and mental-health cases.

trackers.name: Montana Office of the State Public Defender
trackers.desc: Statewide public defender with four divisions and over 20 offices — represents not only people accused of crimes but parents and children in child-welfare cases, elderly people losing autonomy, and people facing civil commitment.

trackers.name: Montana Innocence Project
trackers.desc: Investigates and litigates claims of actual innocence in Montana.

trackers.name: Committee for Public Counsel Services (CPCS)
trackers.desc: Massachusetts’ statewide public defender agency, providing trial and appellate representation through staff attorneys and assigned counsel.

trackers.name: Washington State Office of Public Defense
trackers.desc: Administers and improves indigent defense across Washington State’s trial and appellate courts.

trackers.name: Vermont Office of the Defender General
trackers.desc: Statewide public defense and assigned counsel for Vermonters who cannot afford an attorney.

trackers.name: Virginia Indigent Defense Commission
trackers.desc: Operates Virginia’s public defender offices and sets standards for court-appointed counsel.

trackers.name: Rhode Island Public Defender
trackers.desc: Statewide public defender providing criminal, juvenile and appellate representation.

trackers.name: South Carolina Commission on Indigent Defense
trackers.desc: Administers indigent criminal defense across South Carolina’s circuits.

trackers.name: Justice 360
trackers.desc: South Carolina nonprofit representing people facing the death penalty, serving long sentences, or sentenced as children.

trackers.name: Maryland Office of the Public Defender
trackers.desc: Statewide public defender providing representation in criminal, juvenile and appellate matters.

trackers.name: Michigan State Appellate Defender Office (SADO)
trackers.desc: Provides appellate defense to indigent Michigan defendants and maintains a national criminal defense resource library.

trackers.name: Federal Public Defender — District of Nebraska
trackers.desc: Federal defender office representing people charged with federal crimes in Nebraska who cannot afford counsel.

trackers.name: Federal Public Defender — District of Nevada
trackers.desc: Federal defender office for the District of Nevada.

trackers.name: Rocky Mountain Innocence Center
trackers.desc: Investigates wrongful convictions in Nevada, Utah and Wyoming.

trackers.name: Nashville Office of the Public Defender
trackers.desc: Metropolitan public defender for Nashville and Davidson County.

trackers.name: Georgia Innocence Project
trackers.desc: Nonprofit representing Georgia’s wrongfully convicted — has freed and exonerated 16 people, reviews hundreds of assistance requests each year, and litigates dozens of active innocence cases.

trackers.name: Los Angeles County Public Defender
trackers.desc: The first public defender agency in the United States (est. 1913) and the largest — represents adults, juveniles and people in mental-health proceedings in LA County who cannot afford private counsel. Runs record-clearing clinics across the county.

trackers.name: LA County Alternate Public Defender
trackers.desc: Handles Los Angeles County cases where the Public Defender has a conflict of interest — full criminal defense for indigent clients.

trackers.name: Harris County Public Defender’s Office
trackers.desc: Houston-area public defender (est. 2011) with a client-centered, holistic model and enforced caseload limits — lawyers, investigators and social workers working together; 37% of felony clients leave with no conviction.

trackers.name: Law Office of the Cook County Public Defender
trackers.desc: Chicago’s public defender — one of the largest criminal-defense firms in the US with 450+ attorneys, representing tens of thousands of Cook County residents; runs Freedom Defense Centers connecting clients to community services.

trackers.name: King County Department of Public Defense
trackers.desc: Seattle-area public defense for adults and youth who cannot afford an attorney — 24/7 on-call attorney line, financial screening without appointment, and a Blake Refund Bureau to vacate drug-possession records.

trackers.name: Orleans Public Defenders
trackers.desc: New Orleans public defender representing ~85% of people charged in Orleans Parish (19,487 cases in 2025) — client-centered model with social workers, client advocates and mitigation planning, rebuilt after Hurricane Katrina into a national benchmark.

trackers.name: The Bronx Defenders
trackers.desc: Holistic public defender in the South Bronx (est. 1997) — criminal, civil, family and immigration lawyers plus social workers and advocates address the full fallout of a criminal charge. Winner of the NLADA Clara Shortridge Foltz Award.

trackers.name: Brooklyn Defender Services
trackers.desc: Public defense for Brooklyn (and, since July 2025, Queens criminal cases) — free criminal, family, immigration and civil defense, with advocacy on housing, benefits, education and employment.

trackers.name: Victoria Legal Aid
trackers.desc: Statutory legal aid body for Victoria — duty lawyers at courts and tribunals across the state, a Legal Help phone line and live chat, with services in 34 languages.

trackers.name: Legal Aid NSW
trackers.desc: Statutory legal aid commission for New South Wales, providing criminal, family and civil representation to people who cannot afford a lawyer.

trackers.name: Legal Aid Queensland
trackers.desc: Provides legal assistance to financially disadvantaged Queenslanders, including criminal defence and duty lawyer services.

trackers.name: Legal Aid Western Australia
trackers.desc: Statutory legal aid commission for Western Australia.

trackers.name: Legal Services Commission of South Australia
trackers.desc: South Australia’s statutory legal aid provider, offering criminal defence and legal advice services.

trackers.name: Tasmania Legal Aid
trackers.desc: Statutory legal aid commission for Tasmania.

trackers.name: Legal Aid Northern Territory
trackers.desc: Northern Territory Legal Aid Commission — criminal, family and civil legal assistance.

trackers.name: Legal Aid Ontario
trackers.desc: Provides legal help in English and French to financially eligible low-income Ontarians — criminal duty counsel, certificates for private lawyers, and a Service Finder for local legal clinics and courthouses.

trackers.name: Legal Aid Alberta
trackers.desc: Provincial legal aid for Albertans who cannot afford a lawyer — criminal defence, family and protection matters, with a lawyer directory for applicants.

trackers.name: Legal Aid BC
trackers.desc: Free legal information, advice and representation for BC residents — 34 community offices, Legal Aid Navigators, Parents Legal Centres and First Nations/Indigenous Courts.

trackers.name: Legal Aid Saskatchewan
trackers.desc: Provides criminal and civil legal services to people financially unable to afford counsel, with offices across the province.

trackers.name: Legal Aid Manitoba
trackers.desc: Pays for or helps pay for a lawyer for adults and youth facing criminal charges, and for family and social-benefits matters.

trackers.name: Commission des services juridiques (Québec)
trackers.desc: The agency responsible for ensuring legal aid is provided to every eligible person in Quebec; applications are made at the local legal aid office.

trackers.name: Legal Aid New Brunswick (NBLASC)
trackers.desc: Provides legal assistance to low-income people in criminal and certain family matters; applications are completed at the legal aid office.

trackers.name: Nova Scotia Legal Aid
trackers.desc: Thirteen community-based law offices providing criminal, family and civil legal services; applications can be made online.

trackers.name: Newfoundland and Labrador Legal Aid Commission
trackers.desc: Ensures people with limited financial means have access to legal counsel across Newfoundland and Labrador.

trackers.name: Nunavut Legal Aid (Legal Services Board)
trackers.desc: Legal Services Board of Nunavut — criminal, family and poverty law services across the territory.

trackers.name: NWT Legal Aid Commission
trackers.desc: Oversees legal aid clinics in every administrative region of the Northwest Territories.

trackers.name: Yukon Legal Services Society
trackers.desc: Territorial legal aid for Yukon residents facing criminal charges and family matters.

trackers.name: Scottish Legal Aid Board (SLAB)
trackers.desc: Administers publicly funded legal assistance in Scotland under the Legal Aid (Scotland) Act 1986 — criminal and civil legal aid, eligibility checks and a solicitor finder.

trackers.name: Legal Aid Agency (England & Wales)
trackers.desc: Executive agency of the Ministry of Justice administering the legal aid scheme in England and Wales, and funder of the Public Defender Service.

trackers.name: Legal Aid Agency — Wales
trackers.desc: Check eligibility for legal aid in England and Wales and connect to Civil Legal Advice; Welsh-language advisers available.

trackers.name: Legal Aid SA — Gauteng
trackers.desc: Provincial office list for Legal Aid South Africa in Gauteng — local and satellite offices near the courts they serve, with heads of office and contact details.

trackers.name: Legal Aid SA — Western Cape
trackers.desc: Legal Aid South Africa’s Western Cape local and satellite offices, including court-based access points.

trackers.name: Legal Aid SA — Eastern Cape
trackers.desc: Legal Aid South Africa’s Eastern Cape local and satellite offices with contact details.

trackers.name: Legal Aid SA — KwaZulu-Natal
trackers.desc: Legal Aid South Africa’s KwaZulu-Natal provincial, local and satellite offices — Durban, Pinetown, Empangeni and rural access points, with heads of office and contact details.

trackers.name: Legal Aid SA — Limpopo
trackers.desc: Legal Aid South Africa’s Limpopo offices, including Louis Trichardt, Musina, Modimolle and Lephalale.

trackers.name: Legal Aid SA — Mpumalanga
trackers.desc: Legal Aid South Africa’s Mpumalanga offices — including one inside the Mpumalanga High Court building at Mbombela.

trackers.name: Delhi State Legal Services Authority (DSLSA)
trackers.desc: Constituted under the Legal Services Authorities Act 1987 — free legal services to marginalised people, legal aid for accused persons through the Legal Aid Defense Counsel System and district advocate panels, victim compensation, and Lok Adalats.

trackers.name: Maharashtra State Legal Services Authority (MSLSA)
trackers.desc: State legal services authority giving effect to Articles 14 and 39-A of the Indian Constitution — free legal aid and Lok Adalats under the object “Access to Justice for All”.

trackers.name: Tamil Nadu State Legal Services Authority (TNSLSA)
trackers.desc: Provides free legal aid under Article 39-A so that justice is not denied to any citizen by reason of poverty or disability.

trackers.name: Defensoria Pública do Estado de São Paulo (DPESP)
trackers.desc: Free and comprehensive legal assistance to people who cannot afford a lawyer — criminal defence, judicial and extrajudicial representation in state courts, with unit addresses and a free 0800 booking line.

trackers.name: Defensoria Pública do Estado do Rio de Janeiro (DPRJ)
trackers.desc: Public institution offering free, comprehensive legal assistance and guidance to citizens who cannot pay, and defending human rights and the rights of people in vulnerable situations.
